title: Deep integration: 4 standalone services wired into user-facing endpoints

observation.gap_identified: Survival model (Cox PH), Isolation Forest anomaly detector, Brinson-Fachler attribution + MCTR, and bubble detector were all standalone services with no or partial integration into the main user-facing endpoints

implementation.feature_built: Wired 4 previously standalone services into user-facing endpoints: (1) Cox PH survival model now provides ensemble crash probabilities alongside LightGBM in /api/crash/prediction and /api/crash/{ticker}, (2) Isolation Forest anomaly detection now provides confidence assessment in crash prediction, (3) Brinson-Fachler attribution + MCTR summary now embedded inline in /api/portfolio/analyze (previously required separate API calls), (4) LPPL bubble detection now runs per-stock in /api/stock/{ticker} analysis

limitations: Survival model training happens on each request (should be cached/pre-trained for production)
Bubble detection per-stock depends on price_history being available in the result dict
No new tests written for the integration wiring (covered by existing router tests)
assessment.self_critique: Could have added frontend components to display the new data (bubble indicator widget, survival model comparison chart). The survival model retrains on each crash prediction request which is computationally expensive - should add caching or pre-training.

next_steps: Cache survival model training results (singleton pattern or pickle cache)
Add frontend display for bubble indicator on stock page
Add frontend display for ensemble crash probabilities (LightGBM vs Cox PH comparison)
Display attribution summary and MCTR in portfolio page without requiring separate API call
Wire anomaly detector into stock-level analysis for per-ticker confidence scoring
